gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/397)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/397)

parts.text: أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) أَنَّهُ كَانَ إِذَا أَرَادَ دُخُولَ الْحَمَّامِ تَنَاوَلَ شَيْئاً فَأَكَلَهُ

text: قُلْتُ لَهُ إِنَّ النَّاسَ عِنْدَنَا يَقُولُونَ إِنَّهُ عَلَى الرِّيقِ أَجْوَدُ مَا يَكُونُ قَالَ لَا بَلْ يُؤْكَلُ شَيْ‏ءٌ قَبْلَهُ يُطْفِئُ الْمَرَارَةَ وَ يُسَكِّنُ حَرَارَةَ الْجَوْفِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Rafa’at Bin Musa, from the one who informed it,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> that whenever he<sup>asws</sup> wanted to enter the bathhouse, he<sup>asws</sup> took something, so he<sup>asws</sup> ate it. I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘The people with us are saying that it is much better upon the empty stomach as can be’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No, but eating something before it cools the gallbladder and settles the heat of the interior’.
en.sarwar: 6. Ali ibn al-Hakam from has narrated from Rifa‘ah ibn Musa from those who narrated to him who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, when about to take a bath would eat something. I once asked, ‘People around us say that before eating anything is the best time for taking a bath.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘No, one must eat something before taking bath; it banished bitterness (acidity) and calms down the temperature in the belly.’”
